get.summary: Device Admin - Returns list of library conditions.
get.description: Device Admin - Returns list of library conditions.

parameters.name: X-Request-ID
parameters.description: request Id, will return in the response headers, and appear in logs

404.description: The specified resource was not found

post.summary: Device Admin - Creates a library condition.
post.description:
Device Admin - Creates a library condition:
  • Library Condition has hierarchical structure which define a set of condition for which authentication and authorization policy rules could be match.
  • Condition can be compose from single dictionary attribute name and value using model LibraryConditionAttributes , or from combination of dictionary attributes with logical operation of AND/OR between them, using models: LibraryConditionAndBlock or LibraryConditionOrBlock.
  • When using AND/OR blocks, the condition will include inner layers inside these blocks, these layers are built using the inner condition models: ConditionAttributes, ConditionAndBlock, ConditionOrBlock, that represent dynamically built Conditions which are not stored in the conditions Library, or using ConditionReference, which includes an ID to existing stored condition in the library.
  • The LibraryCondition models can only be used in the outer-most layer (root of the condition) and must always include the condition name.
  • When using one of the 3 inner condition models (ConditionAttributes, ConditionAndBlock, ConditionOrBlock), condition name cannot be included in the request, since these will not be stored in the conditions library, and used only as inner members of the root condition.
  • When using ConditionReference model in inner layers, the condition name is not required.
  • ConditionReference objects can also include a reference ID to a condition of type TimeAndDate.

parameters.name: X-Request-ID
parameters.description: request Id, will return in the response headers, and appear in logs

404.description: Not Found

post.summary: Device Admin - Create a new policy set.
post.description:
Device Admin - Create a new policy set:
  • Policy must include name , service identifier (either server sequence or allowed protocol) and a condition.
  • Condition has hierarchical structure which define a set of condition for which policy could be match.
  • Condition can be either reference to a stored Library condition, using model ConditionReference, or, dynamically built conditions which are not stored in the conditions Library, using models ConditionAttributes, ConditionAndBlock, ConditionOrBlock.

post.summary: Network Access - Create a new policy set.
post.description:
Network Access - Create a new policy set:
  • Policy must include name , service identifier (either server sequence or allowed protocol) and a condition.
  • Condition has hierarchical structure which define a set of condition for which policy could be match.
  • Condition can be either reference to a stored Library condition, using model ConditionReference, or, dynamically built conditions which are not stored in the conditions Library, using models ConditionAttributes, ConditionAndBlock, ConditionOrBlock.

name.description: Command used in Device Admin authorization policies

Condition.title: Condition

conditionType.description:
• Inidicates whether the record is the condition itself(data) or a logical(or,and) aggregation
  • Data type enum(reference,single) indicates than "conditonId" OR "ConditionAttrs" fields should contain condition data but not both
  • Logical aggreation(and,or) enum indicates that additional conditions are present under the children field

isNegate.description: Indicates whereas this condition is in negate mode

Condition.description:
• Hierarchical structure which defines a set of conditions for which authentication or authorization policy rules could be matched.
  • Logical operations(AND, OR) relationship between conditions are supported
  • Each condition can have subconditions with relation to logical operations

ConditionAndBlock.title: ConditionAndBlock

children.description: In case type is andBlock or orBlock addtional conditions will be aggregated under this logical (OR/AND) condition
children.example: [{"name":"Radius condition","description":"Match only radius requests","id":"ad947f38-7063-4da2-8723-652e0ca2ea41","conditionType":"ConditionReference","isNegate":false},{"name":"Tacacs condition","description":"Match only tacacs condition","id":"ad947f38-7063-4da2-8723-652e0ca2eb52","conditionType":"ConditionReference","isNegate":false}]

  • Logical aggreation(and,or) enum indicates that additional conditions are present under the children field

  • Each condition can have subconditions with relation to logical operations

DatesRange.title: DatesRange
DatesRange.description:
Defines for which date/s TimeAndDate condition will be matched
Options are - Date range, for specific date, the same date should be used for start/end date
Default - no specific dates
In order to reset the dates to have no specific dates Date format - yyyy-mm-dd (MM = month, dd = day, yyyy = year)

DeviceCondition.title: DeviceCondition

conditionType.description: This field determines the content of the conditions field

deviceGroupList.description:
This field should contain a tuple with NDG Root, comma, and an NDG (that it under the root).
Line format - NDG Root Name, NDG, Port

deviceList.description: This field should contain Device Name. The device name must be the same as the name field in a Network Device object. Line format - Device Name

ipAddrList.description:
This field should contain IP address or subnet.
IP address can be IPV4 format (n.n.n.n) or IPV6 format (n:n:n:n:n:n:n:n).
IP subnet can be IPV4 format (n.n.n.n/m) or IPV6 format (n:n:n:n:n:n:n:n/m).
Line format - IP Address or subnet

DeviceCondition.description: Unique network conditions to restrict access to the network

DevicePortCondition.title: DevicePortCondition

conditionType.description: This field determines the content of the conditions field

deviceGroupList.description:
This field should contain a tuple with NDG Root, comma, and an NDG (that it under the root).
Line format - NDG Root Name, NDG, Port

deviceList.description:
This field should contain Device-Name,port-number. The device name must be the same as the name field in a Network Device object.
Line format - Device Name,Port

ipAddrList.description:
This field should contain IP-address-or-subnet,port number
IP address can be IPV4 format (n.n.n.n) or IPV6 format (n:n:n:n:n:n:n:n).
IP subnet can be IPV4 format (n.n.n.n/m) or IPV6 format (n:n:n:n:n:n:n:n/m).
Line format - IP Address or subnet,Port

DevicePortCondition.description: Unique network conditions to restrict access to the network

allowedValues.description: all of the allowed values for the dictionary attribute

dataType.description: the data type for the dictionary attribute

description.description: The description of the Dictionary attribute

dictionaryName.description: the name of the dictionary which the dictionary attribute belongs to

directionType.description: the direction for the useage of the dictionary attribute

internalName.description: the internal name of the dictionary attribute

isDefault.description: true if this key value is the default between the allowed values of the dictionary attribute

response.example: [{"allowedValues":[{"key":"key1","value":"value1","isDefault":true}],"dataType":"IPV6","description":"a dictionary attribute description","dictionaryName":"my dictionary","directionType":"BOTH","id":"12345678-f0e7-456b-a2d1-876543320b11","internalName":"my dictionary attribute internal name","name":"my dictionary attribute"}]

EndstationCondition.title: EndstationCondition

cliDnisList.description:
This field should contain a Caller ID (CLI), comma, and Called ID (DNIS).
Line format - Caller ID (CLI), Called ID (DNIS)

conditionType.description: This field determines the content of the conditions field

ipAddrList.description:
This field should contain IP address or subnet.
IP address can be IPV4 format (n.n.n.n) or IPV6 format (n:n:n:n:n:n:n:n).
IP subnet can be IPV4 format (n.n.n.n/m) or IPV6 format (n:n:n:n:n:n:n:n/m).
Line format - IP Address or subnet

macAddrList.description:
This field should contain Endstation MAC address, comma, and Destination MAC addresses.
Each Max address must include twelve hexadecimal digits using formats nn:nn:nn:nn:nn:nn or nn-nn-nn-nn-nn-nn or nnnn.nnnn.nnnn or nnnnnnnnnnnn.
Line format - Endstation MAC,Destination MAC

EndstationCondition.description: Unique network conditions to restrict access to the network

HoursRange.title: HoursRange
HoursRange.description:
Defines for which hours a TimeAndDate condition will be matched
Time foramt - hh:mm ( h = hour , mm = minutes )
Default - All Day

LibraryConditionAndBlock.title: LibraryConditionAndBlock

children.description: In case type is andBlock or orBlock addtional conditions will be aggregated under this logical (OR/AND) condition
children.example: [{"name":"Radius condition","description":"Match only radius requests","id":"ad947f38-7063-4da2-8723-652e0ca2ea41","conditionType":"ConditionReference","isNegate":false},{"name":"Tacacs condition","description":"Match only tacacs condition","id":"ad947f38-7063-4da2-8723-652e0ca2eb52","conditionType":"ConditionReference","isNegate":false}]

conditionType.description:
• Inidicates whether the record is the condition itself(data) or a logical(or,and) aggregation
  • Data type enum(reference,single) indicates than "conditonId" OR "ConditionAttrs" fields should contain condition data but not both
  • Logical aggreation(and,or) enum indicates that additional conditions are present under the children field

isNegate.description: Indicates whereas this condition is in negate mode

name.description: Condition name, [Valid characters are alphanumerics, underscore, hyphen, space]

LibraryConditionAndBlock.description:
• Hierarchical structure which defines a set of conditions for which authentication or authorization policy rules could be matched.
  • Logical operations(AND, OR) relationship between conditions are supported
  • Each condition can have subconditions with relation to logical operations

NetworkCondition.title: NetworkCondition

conditionType.description: This field determines the content of the conditions field

NetworkCondition.description: Unique network conditions to restrict access to the network

default.description: Flag which indicates if this policy set is the default one

description.description: The description for the policy set

hitCounts.description: The amount of times the policy was matched

isProxy.description: Flag which indicates if the policy set service is of type 'Proxy Sequence' or 'Allowed Protocols'

name.description: Given name for the policy set, [Valid characters are alphanumerics, underscore, hyphen, space, period, parentheses]

rank.description: The rank(priority) in relation to other policy set. Lower rank is higher priority.

serviceName.description: Policy set service identifier - Allowed Protocols,Server Sequence..

state.description: The state that the policy set is in. A disabled policy set cannot be matched.

response.example: [{"default":false,"id":"793f55af-ad78-4021-bbdc-6286724394f8","name":"New Policy Set 1","description":null,"hitCounts":0,"rank":0,"state":"enabled","condition":{"link":null,"conditionType":"ConditionReference","isNegate":false,"name":"Switch_Local_Web_Authentication","id":"19d968b0-ebcb-4cdd-98e3-fe02e5521266","description":"A condition to match authentication requests for Local Web Authentication from Cisco Catalyst Switches"},"serviceName":"Default Network Access","isProxy":false,"link":{"rel":"self","href":"https://{{ISE_IP}}/api/v1/policy/{{protocol}}/policy-set/793f55af-ad78-4021-bbdc-6286724394f8","type":"application/json"}}]

RuleAuthentication.title: RuleAuthentication

identitySourceName.description: Identity source name from the identity stores

ifAuthFail.description: Action to perform when authentication fails such as Bad credentials, disabled user and so on

ifProcessFail.description: Action to perform when ISE is uanble to access the identity database

ifUserNotFound.description: Action to perform when user is not found in any of identity stores

RuleAuthentication.description: Rule for authentication in Network Access/Device Admin

commands.description: Command sets enforce the specified list of commands that can be executed by a device administrator

profile.description: Device admin profiles control the initial login session of the device administrator

securityGroup.description: Security group used in authorization policies

default.description: Indicates if this rule is the default one

hitCounts.description: The amount of times the rule was matched

name.description: Rule name, [Valid characters are alphanumerics, underscore, hyphen, space, period, parentheses]

rank.description: The rank(priority) in relation to other rules. Lower rank is higher priority.

state.description: The state that the rule is in. A disabled rule cannot be matched.

RuleMfa.title: RuleMfa

mfaFailAction.description: Action to perform when MFA fails

mfaResultAction.description: Action to perform when MFA is successful

RuleMfa.description: Rule for MFA in Network Access/Device Admin

TimeAndDateCondition.title: TimeAndDateCondition

conditionType.description:
• Inidicates whether the record is the condition itself(data) or a logical(or,and) aggregation
  • Data type enum(reference,single) indicates than "conditonId" OR "ConditionAttrs" fields should contain condition data but not both
  • Logical aggreation(and,or) enum indicates that additional conditions are present under the children field

isNegate.description: Indicates whereas this condition is in negate mode

weekDays.description:
Defines for which days this condition will be matched
Days format - Arrays of WeekDay enums
Default - List of All week days

weekDaysException.description:
Defines for which days this condition will NOT be matched
Days format - Arrays of WeekDay enums
Default - Not enabled

TimeAndDateCondition.description: Condition based on time and date.
